findings.description: No candidate rules were extracted for this blob at all, despite it containing at least five distinct governable design rules (container/panel styling, color usage logic, shape language, headline typography, callout/footnote hierarchy). This is a total extraction miss, not just a clustering issue.

payload.note: Create rule cluster for Charts & Infographics section covering container styling, color logic, shape language, headline typography, and data callout/footnote hierarchy.

findings.description: The color logic statement uses strong governing language ('dominant', 'reserved for ... only') indicating a hard constraint with restrictive polarity and specific scope (data figures/emphasis only vs. neutral placebo/comparator color). No rule exists capturing this hardness/polarity/scope, so this is unrepresented governance.

payload.statement: Brand Blue and Sky Blue must dominate chart color usage; Sunshine and Gold restricted to emphasis on key figures/data points only; placebo/comparator uses neutral gray.

findings.description: Headline rule specifies conditional/channel-scoped typography (Agenda for print vs Nunito Sans Bold+ for email) plus ALL CAPS, color, and rule-line treatment. This is a multi-facet rule with channel-based scope that must not be merged into a generic typography rule without preserving the print/email conditional split.

payload.statement: Headline set in Agenda (print) or Nunito Sans Bold+ (email), ALL CAPS, Brand Blue, thin Gold rule beneath.

findings.description: Statistical callout emphasis (large/bold hero figure) and footnote de-emphasis (smaller, separated) are two sides of the same visual hierarchy relation and should be linked/merged as a single 'data hierarchy' rule rather than left as isolated, unlinked statements once extracted.

payload.statement: Establish visual hierarchy between hero statistical callouts (large, bold, primary) and footnotes (smaller, clearly separated) within charts.

findings.description: Container styling (rounded corners, Light Blue panel, Brand Blue header bar) and rounded bar/icon shapes both express the same 'rounded/approachable' shape language principle; missing relation linking these as reinforcing the same design token (corner radius / approachability) once rules are created.
